Council's functions, not being a purpo.se specified m sub-clause (ii), -— (2) The first mee'mg/ of th<: Coimcil held in any .financial ygar shall be the Annual Meeting of the Council for that year. 5. NOTICE OF MEETINGS 6. 0 ) Notice of every meeting other than a spe- cial meeting called under the proviso to clause (1) of regulation & or under the first proviso to sub-clause (b) of clause (1) of regulation S shall be despat- ched by the Secretary to each member of the Coun- cil not bss than thirty days before the date of the meeting- (2; NoJics of every meeting of the Council shall be rVcspa:ehetf under certificate of posting and noQ-receMit of any notice shall not however, invali- date the proceedings of any meeting, AGENDA PAPERS 7. (1) The Secretary shall issue with tha notice of the meeting a preliminary agenda paper specifying the business to be brought heforc the meeting, the terras of all ir,ofions to be moved of which notice in writing has previously reached him and the names of the movers. (2) A mc-mher who wkhes t.n move any motion not included in the preliminary agenda paper or an amendment to any item so included shall give notice thereof to lb? Secretary not less than fifteen clear 'days before the dst- flsed for the meeting. 2044 Gf/sn—2 (3) The Secretary shall, not less than fifteen clear days before the date fixed for the meeting and in the case of a special meeting with the notice of the meet- ing issue a complete agenda paper specifying business to be brought before the meeting. (4) A member who wishes to move r,n amendment to any item included in the complete agenda paper, •VA-. no: mck'ded in the mcliminary agenda paper, shall give notice thereof to the Secretary not less ihan three clear days before the date fixed for the meeting. (5) The Secretary shall, if time permits, cause a iisi; oi ail amendment oi which notice has been given under clause (4) to be made available for the use of every member : Provided that nothing in this regulation shall pre- vent under special circumstances the reference by the Exe"cutiv2 Committee of any matter to the Council at a meeting following immediately or soon after the meeting of the Executive Committee to permit the notice required under this regulation. ADMISSION OF MOTION S. (1) The President shall disallow any motiou,— (a) if the matter to which it relates is not with- in the scope of the Council's functions; (b) if it raises substantially the same question as a motion cr amendment which has been moved or withdrawn with the leave of the Council, within one year of the date of the meeting at which it i* to be moved : Provided that such a motion may be admitted at a special mee'ing of the Council convened for the pur- pose on the requisition of not less than two thirds of the member.? of the Council : Provided further that nothing in these regulations shall prohibit the further discussion of any master re- ferred to the Council by the Central Government in the exercise cf any of its i'unc'ions under the Act; (c) unless it raises substantially one definite issue; (d) if it con'aios arguments.- inferences, ironical .<• expressions, imputations or defamatory state- men 1s: Provided that if a motion can be rendered admissi- ble by amendment, the President may in lieu of dis- allowing the motion admit it in an amended roni": (2) When the President disallows or amends a motion the Secret aiy shall inform the member who gave the notice of the motion of the order of dis~ cliatvance or, as the cass may bs, of ihe form in which the motion has been admitted. -- 9 of 15 -- in THE GAZETTE OF INDIA ; iXTRAUILDlNARY m?:T ii—ssc. 3u; 3 PAKT -n CONDUCT OF BUSINESS AT MEETINGS OF THE CODKCIL 9, (i) Every meeting of the Council shall be presi- ded over by the President, if he is absent, by the Vice-President or if both the President and tfee Vice- President are absent, by a member to be elected by the members present from amongst themselves. (2) All references in this pari to the President shall be read as referring to the person for the time being presiding over a msermg, 1C- Ti>2 quorum accessary for transaction of busi- ness at a meeting of the Council shall be Nine. If, at the time appointed for a meeting, the quorum is not present, and if the quorum is not present, on the ex- piration of thirty minutes from .the rime appointed for the meeting or during the course of any meeting; the meeiJnsr shall stand adjourned to such future tims and date as the President may appoint. IT (X) Every matter to bs determined - by the Council shall be moved by a member and put to the Council fey the President and shall be decided by a majority of the members present and voting. (2) Votes shall be taken by show of hands or by division or hy "ballot, as the President may direct: Provided that votes shall be taken tiy ballot, if three members so desire and ask for it : f Provided further that, if voting has been by show of hands, a decision shall be taken, if at least one member asks for it. (3) The President shall .determine the method of takm,!* votes by division. (4) The result of the vote shall be announced by the President which shall be final. i (5) In the event of as equality of votes the Presi- dent shall have a casting vote, 12. When motions identical in purport stand m the names of two or mcr;e members, the President shall decide whose motion shall be moved and the other motion or motions shall thereupon be deem- ed to have been withdrawn. 13. (3) Every motion or amendment shall be seconded and-if not seconded shall be deemed to have been withdrawn. (2) When a motion has been seconded, it shall be stated by the President. C3> When a motion has been thus stated, it may be discussed as a question to be resolved either in the affirmative or in the negative, or any member may subject to regulations 14 and 15, move an amendment to the motion: Provided that the President shail not allow an amendment to be moved which, if it has been a sub- stantive motion, would have been inadmissible under regulation 5. 14. (1) An amendment must be relevant to and within the scope of the motion to which it is propos- ed, (2) An amendment may not be moved which has Merely the effect of a negative vote, (3) The President may refuse to put to the Council an amendment which in his opinion is frivolous. 15. A motion may be amended b y - fa) the omission, insertion or addition of word or words; (bj the- substitution of words for any of the ori- ginal words. 16, (1) When a^motion or amendment is ui debate, no proposal with reference thereto shall Be made other than,— (a) an amendment of the motion or of the amendment %$ the case may be, as propos- ed in regulation 13; (b) a motion for the adjournment of the debate on the motion or amendment either to a specified date and hour or sine die; (c) a motion for the closure* namely, a motion that the question be now put; (d) a motion that the Council instead of pro- ceeding to deal with the motion, do pass to the next item on the programme ot busi- ness ; Provided Jhat no such motion of amendment shall be moved so as fa interrupt a spteech : Provided further that no motion of the nature specified in sub-clauses (b;, (c) and (d)f shall be moved or seconded by a member who has "already spoken on the question at the meeting: Provided that no snch motion or amendment shall :;es fe) and (d) shall be moved without a speech. (2) Jt shall be in the discretion of the President to put or refuse to pet to tht Council a proposal of the nature specified in sub-clause (b) of clause (1), (3) Unless the President is of the opinion that a motion for closure is an abuse of the right of reason- able dsbne he shall forthwith put a motion .that the question be now put and if that motion is carried the substantive motion or amendment under debate shall be put forthwith: Provided that the President may allow the mover of the substantive motion to exercise his right of reply before the substantive motion under debate is put. 17. A motion or an amendment which has been moved end zzccndsd shall not be withdrawn save with the leave of the Council which shall not be deemed to be granted, if any member dissents from the grant- in (j of lesv? -- 10 of 15 -- fUtoH—3F* 3(i)j ww m WET •• irensroT n . iS. When a motion has been moved and seconded, members other thm the mover and the seconder may speak on the motion in such order as the President may direct ; Provided that the seconder of a motion or of an amendment may, with the permission of* the President, confine himself to seconding the motion or amend- ment, as the case may be, and speak thereon at aay subsequent stags of Ous debate. 19. During she meeting, the President may, at any time tasks aay objections or suggestions or give in- formation to elucidate any point to help the members in the discussion. 20. (1) The mover of an original motion, and if permitted by the President, the mover of any amend- ment, shall bs entitled to a right of final reply; no other member shall speak more'than once to any de- hate except with pexmission of- the President, for the purpose of making a personal explanation or of putting a question to the member than addressing the Council: Provided that any member at any stage or ih£ debate may rise to a point of order, but no speech shall be allowed on that point : Provided further that a member who has spoken on a motion may $>eak again on the amendment subse- quently moved to the motion, (2) No member shall, save with the permission of the President, speak for more than five minutes. Provided that the mover of a motion when, moving the same may speak for not more than ten minutes, (3) A speech shall be strictly confined to the subject matter of the motion or amendment on which it is made. (4) Any motion or amendment standing m the name of a member who is absent from the meeting or unwilling to move it, may be .brought forward by an- other member with the permission of the President. 21. (1) A member desiring to make any observation on the matter before the Council, shall rise in his place and address the President. (2) If at any time the President rises to speak any member speaking shall immediately resume his seat. 22. No member shall be heard at a meeting except upon t}\e business before the Council. 23. f 1) When an amendment to any motion is mov- ed and seconded or when two or more such amend- ments are mnved and seconded, the President shall, before taking the sense of the Council thereon, state or read to the Council the terms of the original motion and of the amendment or amendments proposed. (2) An amendment to a motion shall be put to the vote first, (3) If there be more th£n one amendment IQ a motion the President shall decide ;n what order thev shnijl bo " 24. When any motion involving several points has been discussed, it shall be in the discretion of the President to divide the motion and put each or any point separately to the vote as he may think fit. 25. (1) The President may, if he deems necessary QUA to exigencies, at any tmie, adjourn any meeting to aay future day or to any hour of the same day snd state'the reasons therefor. (2) Whenever, a meeting is adjourned to a future day, the Secretary .shall, send notice of the adjourn- ment to every member who was not present at such meeting. (3) When a meeting has been adjourned to a future day the President may change such day to any other day fox compelling reasons and the Secretary shall send written notice of the change to each member. (4) At 3 meeting adjourned to a future day any motion standing over from the previous day shall, unless the President otherwise directs, take precedence over other matters on the agenda, (5) The President or a member may suggest a change in the order of business on the agenda, either at the beginning of the meeting or after the conclusion of the debate on a particular item during the meeting and If the Council agrees, such a change shall be made. (6) No matter which nad not been on the agenda of the original meeting shall be discussed at an ad- journed meeting. (7) The same quorum shall be necessary for an adjourned meeting as for ah ordinary meeting. 26. (1) The President shall decide all points of order which -may arise, and his decision shall he final. (2) Jf any question, arises with reference to pro- cedure in respect of a matter for which these regula- tion make no provision, the President shall decide the, same and .his decision thereon shall be .final, (3) The President shall direct any business, which may be necessary for the Council or the Executive Committee to discuss and decide, to be transacted by circulation among the members of the Council or the Executive Committee as the case may be : Provided that if ten members of the Council or three members of the Executive Committee, as the case may be, desire that any particular subject shall be decided at a meeting instead of by circulation, it shall be placed before a meeting of the Council or the Executive Committee, (4) Any resolution or report which is circulated as stated above and approved by a majority of the mem- bers signing shall be binding as a resolution adopted, as if, in a meeting of the Council or the Executive Committee. 27. Four representatives of the Press at the discre- tion of the^ President. and other invitees, not exceeding four at a time, may be admitted to a meeting on pro- duction of permits from the Secretary, The representa- tive s chs!! be required to obtain the previous approval h -- 11 of 15 -- 12 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA: of the Secrs;ary to the publication of their report uf the proceeding. PART 111 MINUTES OF THE COUNCIL 28, The proceedings of the meetings of the Counc0 shall be preserved in tne form of printed minutes which snail be authenticated, after coiifrrmaiion, by the signa- ture of the President, 29, A copy of the minutes of each meeting shall be submitted to the President within ten days of the meeting and attested by him and they shall then be sent to each member within thirty days of the meet- ing- 30, The minutes of each meeting shall contain such motions and amendments as have been moved and adopted or negatived with the name of the mover and the seconder, but without any ccmrnem and. without any record of observations made by any member at the meeting- 31, If any objection regarding the correctness of the minutes is received within thirty days of the despatch of the minutes by the Secretary such objection toge- ther with the minutes as recorded and attested shall be put before the next meeting of the Council for confir- mation. At this meeting no question shall be raised excet as the con'ec'ness of the records of the meeting-. Provided that if no objection regarding a decision taken by the Council at a meeting is received within thirty days of the despatch by the Secretary of the minutes of that particular meeting, such decision may, if expedient, be put into effect before the confirmation of tbe minutes at the next meeting : i Provided further that the President may direct that action may be taken on a decision of the Council be- fore' tbe expiry of the period of thirty days specified above. j 32. PART V RESIGNATION AND FILLING OF CASUAL VACANCIES 56. A member desiring to resign hts seat on the Council shall send his resignation in writing to the President and his resignation shall take effect from the date on which it is accepted by the President or on the expiry of ninety days from the date of receipt of his letter to President, whichever is earlier, after con- firmation from tbe member concerned. 37, When a casual vacancy by reason of death, resignation of a member or cessation of membership in accordance with section 6 of the Act, a report shall be made forthwith by the President to the Central Go- vernment for necessary action to fill up the vacancy, subject to proviso of section 7 of the Act. PART VI THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE-MEMBERSHIP, FUNCTIONS, MEETINGS ETC. 33. The Executive Commit:..,; shall have Nine mem- bers including the President, the Vice-President and the Secretary of the Council who shall act as Presi- dent, Vice-P'resiuent rmd Secretary respec'ivefy. 39, The meetings of the Executive Committee shall te generally governed by the regulations applicable to the meetings of the Council. 40.
